evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Design clean, reliable, and well-documented data models as a single source of truth
evidence.domain_risk: Own and evolve core business metrics - from definition to tracking and operationalisation
evidence.data_team_maturity: Build and maintain scalable data pipelines and data marts using modern tooling
evidence.jd_authorship: Conduct analyses to uncover insights and inform strategic decisions
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Own and evolve core business metrics - from definition to tracking and operationalisation; Develop the data and analytics components of the AI stack to support experimentation and GenAI applications
evidence.autonomy_level: Own and evolve core business metrics - from definition to tracking and operationalisation
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Build and maintain scalable data pipelines and data marts using modern tooling
evidence.ai_role: Develop the data and analytics components of the AI stack to support experimentation and GenAI applications
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate builds AI-ready, governed data foundations that GenAI applications and experimentation consume, which is infrastructure enablement for AI systems rather than personal AI tool use.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: The JD emphasizes reliable, well-documented data models and metric ownership but never names a testing tool or frames testing as an explicit owned responsibility.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Design clean, reliable, and well-documented data models as a single source of truth
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: The emphasis on reliability and governed 'single source of truth' data reflects an operational trust concern secondary to metric ownership and AI-enablement delivery, without explicit regulatory or audit framing.
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: The JD emphasizes reliability, documentation quality, and governance (single source of truth) with no velocity-oriented language like "fast-paced" or "ship quickly".
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: While Getsafe operates in the regulated insurance sector, the JD frames the role as business analytics without explicitly invoking financial reporting, compliance, or regulatory consequences.
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: The emphasis on maintaining and scaling existing infrastructure with named tools (dbt, Metabase) indicates a maturing team with established but evolving systems, not a greenfield build or fully mature specialized division.
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Most responsibilities use generic boilerplate action verbs that could apply to any analytics role; while AI/GenAI applications are mentioned, there are no scale numbers, specific metrics, or precise tool application contexts typical of hiring-manager authorship.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The first two responsibilities equally emphasize business metric ownership and product/engineering AI enablement, with no clear primary audience across the responsibilities section.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The verb 'own' combined with 'from definition to tracking' indicates end-to-end ownership and the autonomy to define and shape how core metrics are structured and evolved, rather than executing direction from others.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Both build/design (new work) and maintain/evolve/scale (existing systems) verbs present without clear dominance.
